_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform
actual_score: Brazil 1-2 Norway (upset -- Norway were 20% pre-match underdogs)

question_results.text: Will Vinícius Júnior (Brazil) score a goal?
Will Erling Haaland (Norway) score a goal?
question_results.note: Bumped above market on knockout-stakes regime-coverage reasoning (scored in all 3 appearances incl. R32); validated.

question_results.text: Will Martin Ødegaard (Norway) score or assist a goal?
Will Matheus Cunha (Brazil) have 1 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Both us and crowd wrong-direction; we were marginally closer.

question_results.text: Will Brazil advance to the quarterfinals?
question_results.note: Brazil lost the upset; market-based number reflected genuine ~30% risk that materialized -- not a methodology failure.

question_results.text: Will the match be decided by a penalty shootout?
Will the match finish with exactly 2 total goals?
Will Brazil have 6 or more corner kicks?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Will Norway have 4 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Trusted the direct market read (exact line match) with no override -- correct.

question_results.text: Will the match be tied at halftime?
Will the second half produce more goals than the first half?
Will Brazil have 6 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: MAJOR VALIDATION of WINNING_PATTERNS_SYNTHESIS.md Cluster-B discipline: deliberately stayed at market (0.41) instead of blending toward Brazil's own empirical SOT average (0.631, which would have been wrong). Correct call, second-biggest win of the match.

question_results.text: Will both teams receive at least one card?
question_results.note: BIGGEST WIN OF THE MATCH, and one of the largest single-question wins of the campaign. Priced via independent per-team Poisson model using each team's OWN real card rate (Brazil 1.75/game, Norway only 0.5/game) rather than a generic symmetric assumption: P(BRA>=1)=0.826 x P(NOR>=1)=0.393 = 0.325. Crowd's 62% looks like a generic 'both teams get carded in a big knockout match' heuristic that ignored Norway's genuinely low personal disciplinary record. See chat analysis for full breakdown.

key_lessons: 13/15 beat crowd -- best beat-crowd rate of any match this campaign. Both losses were small (-5.06, -5.47) and both were 'right process, market moved against us' cases, not methodology failures.

Q15 (both teams carded, +59.43) validates a NEW pattern: independent per-team Poisson modeling using each team's actual historical rate, for 'both teams do X' compound questions, can massively beat a crowd that over-generalizes compound probabilities.

Q14 (Brazil 6+ SOT, +22.57) is the first live confirmation that the WINNING_PATTERNS_SYNTHESIS.md Cluster-B discipline (stay at market, don't blend a favorite's SOT threshold upward) is worth keeping as a standing rule.
